title: URLs: Website Addresses
drDigitalIntro: Every website has an address called a URL (Uniform Resource Locator). It starts with http:// or https:// and looks like: www.google.com or www.wikipedia.org. The address tells your browser where to go on the internet. Type a URL in Safari's address bar and press Return — Safari takes you there. You might see URLs written on signs, in emails, or in books: click them or type them to visit.
drDigitalSuccess: You understand how URLs work — they're website addresses!
drDigitalHint: https:// at the start means the website is secure.
